channel.title: Daily GovCon Briefing
channel.description: Daily synthesis of federal contracting news — acquisition policy, DoD and VA, federal AI policy — and what it means for contractors. Five to seven minutes. One voice. Weekdays.
channel.itunes:author: Mike Ross
channel.itunes:summary: Daily synthesis of federal contracting news and what it means for contractors.

item.title: GovCon today: June 11, 2026 — The Sole-Source AI Reflex — and Who It Locks Out
item.description: The federal government is generating AI-related mission requirements faster than it is building the acquisition infrastructure to fill them competitively. Today's ICE sole-source to TRM Labs — up to $100 million, FFP, justified under Revolutionary FAR Overhaul provisions — is the clearest single expression of that gap, and a template agencies will repeat. Small AI shops not already in pre-solicitation conversations are structurally invisible to the awards that matter most right now.
item.content:encoded:
The Sole-Source AI Reflex — and Who It Locks Out

The federal government is generating AI-related mission requirements faster than it is building the acquisition infrastructure to fill them competitively. Today's ICE sole-source to TRM Labs — up to $100 million, FFP, justified under Revolutionary FAR Overhaul provisions — is the clearest single expression of that gap, and a template agencies will repeat. Small AI shops not already in pre-solicitation conversations are structurally invisible to the awards that matter most right now.

Featured story

  • ICE Eyes Sole-Source Award for Homeland Security Task Force Analytical Support — GovConWire

Also today

  • Lack of White House guidance has complicated agency Mythos adoption, people familiar say — Washington Technology
  • CISA directive revamps how agencies prioritize vulnerable systems — Nextgov/FCW
  • Innovation on ice: How DOGE derailed an SBA tech program — FedScoop
  • Oracle wins contract for first-ever governmentwide HR system — FedScoop
  • OPM's long-planned HR overhaul moves ahead with $396M award to Oracle — Government Executive
  • Oracle wins $396M federal HR systems overhaul contract — Nextgov/FCW
  • OPM awards major HR IT modernization contract to Oracle — Federal News Network
  • NIH pulls the plug on its government-wide contracts — Washington Technology
  • GSA gets moving on second batch of Polaris SDVOSB awards — Washington Technology
  • House appropriators release $1 trillion defense bill for FY27 — Breaking Defense
  • Trump's return-to-work mandate for feds helped drive MetTel's GSA upgrades — Nextgov/FCW
  • CISA directive orders agencies to prioritize vulnerability patching in a new way — FedScoop
  • AI directive focuses patching efforts on 'highest risk' vulnerabilities — Federal News Network
